text.title: Topical Authority via Topic Clusters
text.summary: Build topical authority through the pillar-cluster model: one comprehensive pillar page plus supporting subtopic articles, all internally linked.

rule.statement: Structure content as topic clusters: one pillar page (broad overview of the topic) supported by multiple cluster articles (specific subtopics), with bidirectional internal links between them.
rule.rationale: Google's algorithms assess topical coverage, not just individual keyword optimization. A cluster of 10-30 interlinking articles on RAG signals deeper expertise than a single article — even one highly optimized for the target keyword.

example.note: Pillar: "Complete Guide to RAG" linking to cluster articles: "What is RAG?", "RAG vs Fine-tuning", "RAG Architecture Patterns", "RAG Evaluation Methods", "RAG Tools Comparison", "RAG Case Studies"

antipattern.description: Publishing standalone articles targeting individual keywords without cluster structure
antipattern.reason: Without topical coverage depth, individual articles compete against established authorities that already cover the full topic — a fight you lose unless your domain has equivalent overall authority.

rule.statement: Answer all related questions, cover edge cases, and update content regularly — comprehensive coverage signals topical expertise more than article length alone.
rule.rationale: Google's "helpful content" evaluation rewards content that fully satisfies user intent. Thin cluster articles that repeat what the pillar already covers dilute rather than amplify authority.

list.item: Pillar page: comprehensive overview, 2000+ words, links to all cluster articles
Cluster articles: deep coverage of specific subtopic, link back to pillar and related clusters
Answer "People Also Ask" questions within the cluster
Cover edge cases and advanced scenarios in dedicated cluster articles
Update cluster articles when the topic evolves — stale content degrades authority
Quality backlinks to pillar and cluster pages
Author credentials and expertise signals (About page, author bios)

rule.statement: Link from pillar to all cluster articles; link from each cluster article back to the pillar and to related clusters; use keyword-rich anchor text that describes the destination content.
rule.rationale: Internal linking distributes PageRank across the cluster and signals to Google that these pages form a related content group. Anchor text reinforces keyword relevance without the manipulation risk of external anchor text over-optimization.
